subject: Mathematics
chapter_name: Another Peek Beyond the Point (Solid Shapes)
overall_learning_objectives: Verify and apply Euler’s Formula (F + V - E = 2)
Visualize and identify Cross-sections of solids
Interpret different views: Front, Side, and Top (Plan) views
Develop spatial reasoning for complex/composite solids

lesson_plan.title: Euler’s Formula and Slicing Solids
topics_covered: Deriving and checking F + V = E + 2
Predicting shapes of cross-sections (horizontal vs. vertical)
Visualizing the internal geometry of solids
student_activities: Use Euler's formula to find the missing variable (e.g., If F=5, V=6, find E)
Draw the cross-section resulting from a vertical slice of a cylinder

lesson_plan.title: Shadows and Multiple Views
topics_covered: Drawing Front, Side, and Top views of composite solids
teaching_strategy: Viewing objects through a 'viewfinder' or box
[Image showing shadows of a cylinder from different light angles]
student_activities: Sketch the Top, Front, and Side views of a staircase model made of cubes
Match a set of shadows to their possible 3D objects

teacher_tip: To master multi-view drawings, tell students to imagine they are an ant looking at the object from the front, a bird looking from the top, and a beetle looking from the side.
